title: Cacalia stricta
intro: Cacalia stricta, also known as Korean root or Asian artichoke, is a perennial plant species in the family Asteraceae, native to East Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities that can aid in relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and protecting against infections. The bioactive constituents of Cacalia stricta include saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Cacalia stricta is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into tonics and supplements to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Cacalia stricta is classified within the family Compositae, commonly referred to as the daisy or sunflower family. The genus Cacalia is part of the tribe Cacalieae, a group of flowering plants that share similar morphological features.
